NKJV.1: Therefore, when we could no longer endure it, we thought it good to be left in Athens alone,
NKJV.2: and sent Timothy, our brother and minister of God, and our fellow laborer in the gospel of Christ, to establish you and encourage you concerning your faith,
NKJV.3: that no one should be shaken by these afflictions; for you yourselves know that we are appointed to this.
NKJV.4: For, in fact, we told you before when we were with you that we would suffer tribulation, just as it happened, and you know.
NKJV.5: For this reason, when I could no longer endure it, I sent to know your faith, lest by some means the tempter had tempted you, and our labor might be in vain.
NKJV.6: But now that Timothy has come to us from you, and brought us good news of your faith and love, and that you always have good remembrance of us, greatly desiring to see us, as we also to see you—
NKJV.7: therefore, brethren, in all our affliction and distress we were comforted concerning you by your faith.
NKJV.8: For now we live, if you stand fast in the Lord.
NKJV.9: For what thanks can we render to God for you, for all the joy with which we rejoice for your sake before our God,
NKJV.10: night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face and perfect what is lacking in your faith?
NKJV.11: Now may our God and Father Himself, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way to you.
NKJV.12: And may the Lord make you increase and abound in love to one another and to all, just as we do to you,
NKJV.13: so that He may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all His saints.

ESV.1: Therefore when we could bear it no longer, we were willing to be left behind at Athens alone,
ESV.2: and we sent Timothy, our brother and God's coworker  in the gospel of Christ, to establish and exhort you in your faith,
ESV.3: that no one be moved by these afflictions. For you yourselves know that we are destined for this.
ESV.4: For when we were with you, we kept telling you beforehand that we were to suffer affliction, just as it has come to pass, and just as you know.
ESV.5: For this reason, when I could bear it no longer, I sent to learn about your faith, for fear that somehow the tempter had tempted you and our labor would be in vain.
ESV.6: But now that Timothy has come to us from you, and has brought us the good news of your faith and love and reported that you always remember us kindly and long to see us, as we long to see you —
ESV.7: for this reason, brothers,  in all our distress and affliction we have been comforted about you through your faith.
ESV.8: For now we live, if you are standing fast in the Lord.
ESV.9: For what thanksgiving can we return to God for you, for all the joy that we feel for your sake before our God,
ESV.10: as we pray most earnestly night and day that we may see you face to face and supply what is lacking in your faith?
ESV.11: Now may our God and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus, direct our way to you,
ESV.12: and may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all, as we do for you,
ESV.13: so that he may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.

NASB.1: Therefore when we could endure [it] no longer, we thought it best to be left behind at Athens alone,
NASB.2: and we sent Timothy, our brother and God's fellow worker in the gospel of Christ, to strengthen and encourage you as to your faith,
NASB.3: so that no one would be disturbed by these afflictions; for you yourselves know that we have been destined for this.
NASB.4: For indeed when we were with you, we [kept] telling you in advance that we were going to suffer affliction; and so it came to pass, as you know.
NASB.5: For this reason, when I could endure [it] no longer, I also sent to find out about your faith, for fear that the tempter might have tempted you, and our labor would be in vain.
NASB.6: But now that Timothy has come to us from you, and has brought us good news of your faith and love, and that you always think kindly of us, longing to see us just as we also long to see you,
NASB.7: for this reason, brethren, in all our distress and affliction we were comforted about you through your faith;
NASB.8: for now we [really] live, if you stand firm in the Lord.
NASB.9: For what thanks can we render to God for you in return for all the joy with which we rejoice before our God on your account,
NASB.10: as we night and day keep praying most earnestly that we may see your face, and may complete what is lacking in your faith?
NASB.11: Now may our God and Father Himself and Jesus our Lord direct our way to you;
NASB.12: and may the Lord cause you to increase and abound in love for one another, and for all people, just as we also [do] for you;
NASB.13: so that He may establish your hearts without blame in holiness before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all His saints.

NLT.1: Finally, when we could stand it no longer, we decided to stay alone in Athens,
NLT.2: and we sent Timothy to visit you. He is our brother and God’s co-worker in proclaiming the Good News of Christ. We sent him to strengthen you, to encourage you in your faith,
NLT.3: and to keep you from being shaken by the troubles you were going through. But you know that we are destined for such troubles.
NLT.4: Even while we were with you, we warned you that troubles would soon come — and they did, as you well know.
NLT.5: That is why, when I could bear it no longer, I sent Timothy to find out whether your faith was still strong. I was afraid that the tempter had gotten the best of you and that our work had been useless.
NLT.6: But now Timothy has just returned, bringing us good news about your faith and love. He reports that you always remember our visit with joy and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you.
NLT.7: So we have been greatly encouraged in the midst of our troubles and suffering, dear brothers and sisters, because you have remained strong in your faith.
NLT.8: It gives us new life to know that you are standing firm in the Lord.
NLT.9: How we thank God for you! Because of you we have great joy as we enter God’s presence.
NLT.10: Night and day we pray earnestly for you, asking God to let us see you again to fill the gaps in your faith.
NLT.11: May God our Father and our Lord Jesus bring us to you very soon.
NLT.12: And may the Lord make your love for one another and for all people grow and overflow, just as our love for you overflows.
NLT.13: May he, as a result, make your hearts strong, blameless, and holy as you stand before God our Father when our Lord Jesus comes again with all his holy people. Amen.

WEB.1: Therefore when we couldn’t stand it any longer, we thought it good to be left behind at Athens alone,
WEB.2: and sent Timothy, our brother and God’s servant in the Good News of Christ, to establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith;
WEB.3: that no one be moved by these afflictions. For you know that we are appointed to this task.
WEB.4: For most certainly, when we were with you, we told you beforehand that we are to suffer affliction, even as it happened, and you know.
WEB.5: For this cause I also, when I couldn’t stand it any longer, sent that I might know your faith, for fear that by any means the tempter had tempted you, and our labor would have been in vain.
WEB.6: But when Timothy came just now to us from you, and brought us glad news of your faith and love, and that you have good memories of us always, longing to see us, even as we also long to see you;
WEB.7: for this cause, brothers, we were comforted over you in all our distress and affliction through your faith.
WEB.8: For now we live, if you stand fast in the Lord.
WEB.9: For what thanksgiving can we render again to God for you, for all the joy with which we rejoice for your sakes before our God;
WEB.10: night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face, and may perfect that which is lacking in your faith?
WEB.11: Now may our God and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way to you;
WEB.12: and the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another, and toward all men, even as we also do toward you,
WEB.13: to the end he may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.

CSB17.1: Therefore, when we could no longer stand it, we thought it was better to be left alone in Athens. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: And we sent Timothy, <sup>ⓖ</sup> our brother and God’s coworker <sup>[1]</sup> in the gospel of Christ, <sup>ⓗ</sup> to strengthen and encourage you concerning your faith,
CSB17.3: so that no one will be shaken by these afflictions. For you yourselves know that we are appointed to this.
CSB17.4: In fact, when we were with you, we told you in advance that we were going to experience affliction, and as you know, it happened.
CSB17.5: For this reason, when I could no longer stand it, I also sent him to find out about your faith, fearing that the tempter had tempted you and that our labor might be for nothing.
CSB17.6: But now Timothy has come to us <sup>ⓘ</sup> from you and brought us good news about your faith and love. He reported that you always have good memories of us and that you long to see us, as we also long to see you. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.7: Therefore, brothers and sisters, in all our distress and affliction, we were encouraged about you through your faith.
CSB17.8: For now we live, if you stand firm <sup>ⓚ</sup> in the Lord.
CSB17.9: How can we thank God for you in return for all the joy <sup>ⓛ</sup> we experience before our God because of you,
CSB17.10: as we pray very earnestly night and day to see you face to face <sup>ⓜ</sup> and to complete what is lacking in your faith? <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.11: Now may our God and Father <sup>ⓐ</sup> himself, and our Lord Jesus, <sup>ⓑ</sup> direct our way to you.
CSB17.12: And may the Lord cause you to increase and overflow with love <sup>ⓒ</sup> for one another <sup>ⓓ</sup> and for everyone, just as we do for you.
CSB17.13: May he make your hearts blameless <sup>ⓔ</sup> in holiness <sup>ⓕ</sup> before our God and Father at the coming <sup>ⓖ</sup> of our Lord <sup>ⓗ</sup> Jesus with all his saints. Amen. <sup>[2]</sup>

AMP.1: Therefore, when we could no longer endure our separation [from you], we thought it best to be left behind, alone at Athens,
AMP.2: and so we sent Timothy, our brother and God's servant in [spreading] the good news of Christ, to strengthen and encourage you [exhorting, comforting, and establishing you] in regard to your faith,
AMP.3: so that no one would be unsettled by these difficulties [to which I have referred]. For you know that we have been destined for this [as something unavoidable in our position].
AMP.4: For even when we were with you, we warned you plainly in advance that we were going to experience persecution; and so, as you know, it has come to pass.
AMP.5: For this reason, when I could no longer endure the suspense, I sent someone to find out about your faith [how you were holding up under pressure], for fear that somehow the tempter had tempted you and our work [among you] would prove to be ineffective.
AMP.6: But now that Timothy has come back to us from [his visit with] you, and has brought us good news of your [steadfast] faith and [the warmth of your] love, and [reported] that you always think kindly of us and treasure your memories of us, longing to see us just as we long to see you,
AMP.7: for this reason, brothers and sisters, during all our distress and suffering we have been comforted and greatly encouraged about you because of your faith [your unwavering trust in God—placing yourselves completely in His loving hands];
AMP.8: because now we really live [in spite of everything], if you stand firm in the Lord.
AMP.9: For what [adequate] thanks can we offer to God for you in return for all the joy and delight we have before our God on your account?
AMP.10: We continue to pray night and day most earnestly that we may see you face to face, and may complete whatever may be imperfect and lacking in your faith.
AMP.11: Now may our God and Father Himself, and Jesus our Lord guide our steps to you [by removing the obstacles that stand in our way].
AMP.12: And may the Lord cause you to increase and excel and overflow in love for one another, and for all people, just as we also do for you;
AMP.13: so that He may strengthen and establish your hearts without blame in holiness in the sight of our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all His saints (God's people).

MSG.1: So when we couldn't stand being separated from you any longer and could find no way to visit you ourselves, we stayed in Athens
MSG.2: and sent Timothy to get you up and about, cheering you on so you wouldn't be discouraged by these hard times. He's a brother and companion in the faith, God's man in spreading the Message, preaching Christ.
MSG.3: Not that the troubles should come as any surprise to you. You've always known that we're in for this kind of thing. It's part of our calling.
MSG.4: When we were with you, we made it quite clear that there was trouble ahead. And now that it's happened, you know what it's like.
MSG.5: That's why I couldn't quit worrying; I had to know for myself how you were doing in the faith. I didn't want the Tempter getting to you and tearing down everything we had built up together.
MSG.6: But now that Timothy is back, bringing this terrific report on your faith and love, we feel a lot better. It's especially gratifying to know that you continue to think well of us, and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you!
MSG.7: In the middle of our trouble and hard times here, just knowing how you're doing keeps us going.
MSG.8: Knowing that your faith is alive keeps us alive.
MSG.9: What would be an adequate thanksgiving to offer God for all the joy we experience before him because of you?
MSG.10: We do what we can, praying away, night and day, asking for the bonus of seeing your faces again and doing what we can to help when your faith falters.
MSG.11: May God our Father himself and our Master Jesus clear the road to you!
MSG.12: And may the Master pour on the love so it fills your lives and splashes over on everyone around you, just as it does from us to you.
MSG.13: May you be infused with strength and purity, filled with confidence in the presence of God our Father when our Master Jesus arrives with all his followers.

NIV.1: So when we could stand it no longer, we thought it best to be left by ourselves in Athens.
NIV.2: We sent Timothy, who is our brother and God’s fellow worker in spreading the gospel of Christ, to strengthen and encourage you in your faith,
NIV.3: so that no one would be unsettled by these trials. You know quite well that we were destined for them.
NIV.4: In fact, when we were with you, we kept telling you that we would be persecuted. And it turned out that way, as you well know.
NIV.5: For this reason, when I could stand it no longer, I sent to find out about your faith. I was afraid that in some way the tempter might have tempted you and our efforts might have been useless.
NIV.6:
Timothy’s Encouraging Report
But Timothy has just now come to us from you and has brought good news about your faith and love. He has told us that you always have pleasant memories of us and that you long to see us, just as we also long to see you.
NIV.7: Therefore, brothers, in all our distress and persecution we were encouraged about you because of your faith.
NIV.8: For now we really live, since you are standing firm in the Lord.
NIV.9: How can we thank God enough for you in return for all the joy we have in the presence of our God because of you?
NIV.10: Night and day we pray most earnestly that we may see you again and supply what is lacking in your faith.
NIV.11: Now may our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus clear the way for us to come to you.
NIV.12: May the Lord make your love increase and overflow for each other and for everyone else, just as ours does for you.
NIV.13: May he strengthen your hearts so that you will be blameless and holy in the presence of our God and Father when our Lord Jesus comes with all his holy ones.

NRSVCE.1: Therefore when we could bear it no longer, we decided to be left alone in Athens;
NRSVCE.2: and we sent Timothy, our brother and co-worker for God in proclaiming the gospel of Christ, to strengthen and encourage you for the sake of your faith,
NRSVCE.3: so that no one would be shaken by these persecutions. Indeed, you yourselves know that this is what we are destined for.
NRSVCE.4: In fact, when we were with you, we told you beforehand that we were to suffer persecution; so it turned out, as you know.
NRSVCE.5: For this reason, when I could bear it no longer, I sent to find out about your faith; I was afraid that somehow the tempter had tempted you and that our labor had been in vain.
NRSVCE.6: But Timothy has just now come to us from you, and has brought us the good news of your faith and love. He has told us also that you always remember us kindly and long to see us — just as we long to see you.
NRSVCE.7: For this reason, brothers and sisters, during all our distress and persecution we have been encouraged about you through your faith.
NRSVCE.8: For we now live, if you continue to stand firm in the Lord.
NRSVCE.9: How can we thank God enough for you in return for all the joy that we feel before our God because of you?
NRSVCE.10: Night and day we pray most earnestly that we may see you face to face and restore whatever is lacking in your faith.
NRSVCE.11: Now may our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus direct our way to you.
NRSVCE.12: And may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all, just as we abound in love for you.
NRSVCE.13: And may he so strengthen your hearts in holiness that you may be blameless before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.

NET.1: So when we could bear it no longer, we decided to stay on in Athens alone.
NET.2: We sent Timothy, our brother and fellow worker for God in the gospel of Christ, to strengthen you and encourage you about your faith,
NET.3: so that no one would be shaken by these afflictions. For you yourselves know that we are destined for this.
NET.4: For in fact when we were with you, we were telling you in advance that we would suffer affliction, and so it has happened, as you well know.
NET.5: So when I could bear it no longer, I sent to find out about your faith, for fear that the tempter somehow tempted you and our toil had proven useless.
NET.6: But now Timothy has come to us from you and given us the good news of your faith and love and that you always think of us with affection and long to see us just as we also long to see you!
NET.7: So in all our distress and affliction, we were reassured about you, brothers and sisters, through your faith.
NET.8: For now we are alive again, if you stand firm in the Lord.
NET.9: For how can we thank God enough for you, for all the joy we feel because of you before our God?
NET.10: We pray earnestly night and day to see you in person and make up what may be lacking in your faith.
NET.11: Now may God our Father himself and our Lord Jesus direct our way to you.
NET.12: And may the Lord cause you to increase and abound in love for one another and for all, just as we do for you,
NET.13: so that your hearts are strengthened in holiness to be blameless before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.
